marketFocus: Operation Epic Fury Day 6: APAC staged a strong comeback (+KOSPI +11%) after Wall St Nasdaq-led rally (+1.51%); Iran's missile launch rate declining — US/Israel claim 70% reduction in launch volumes; Iran denied NYT report of CIA outreach, but geopolitical off-ramp narrative briefly boosted risk assets and crushed crude/nat gas

China NPC: 2026 GDP target 4.5%-5.0% (slowest since 1991), CPI ~2%, CNY 800bln new policy tools — PBoC USD/CNY fix at strongest in ~3 years; bullish for AUD/NZD via China demand channel; EU expects exemption from US 15% universal tariff

BoJ sources (Bloomberg): April rate hike not ruled out, little chance of March hike — USD/JPY choppy 156.85-157.86; Swiss CPI Feb +0.6% MoM (vs +0.5% exp) hot — SNB Vice Chair reiterates FX intervention readiness; ECB Morgan Stanley now expects NO cuts in 2026 (prev. 2 cuts); Goldman Sachs pushes next BoE cut to April

USD.summary: DXY eked mild gains after softening Wednesday as Iran-CIA peace rumour (later denied) briefly weighed on the Dollar. DXY traded 98.700-99.329 range — well inside Tuesday's 98.435-99.683 band. ADP beat (63K vs 50K exp) and ISM Services beat (56.1 vs 53.5 exp) confirm US economic resilience. Fed speakers (Miran, Hammack) both say too early to assess Iran inflation impact; policy well-positioned. Today's key event: US Unemployment Claims (14:30 Frankfurt). War premium remains intact — US Senate blocked war powers resolution 53-47.
drivers: ADP Non-Farm Feb 63K (vs 50K exp, beat) — labour market resilient
ISM Services PMI 56.1 (vs 53.5 exp, strong beat) — services expansion
DXY 98.70-99.33 range — Iran peace rumour (denied) briefly softened USD
Fed Miran: 100bps of easing appropriate in 2026, March cut appropriate
US Unemployment Claims today (14:30) — NFP preview catalyst

EUR.summary: EUR/USD eked mild gains above 1.1600 (range 1.1575-1.1655) as European bourses rallied +1.5% on the Iran-CIA peace rumour. However, the recovery is fragile — Morgan Stanley now expects ECB to hold rates flat through all of 2026 (vs prior 2 cuts), citing energy shock stagflation risk. EU expects exemption from US 15% tariff (positive), but Trump's Spain trade threat remains an overhang. Final Services PMI 51.9 (in-line) and EU Unemployment 6.1% (beat vs 6.2% exp) are stale data. ECB Lagarde speaks today — key for guidance on energy shock response.
drivers: EUR/USD 1.1575-1.1655 range — fragile above 1.1600 support
Morgan Stanley: ECB to hold all 2026 — energy shock stagflation risk
EU expects exemption from US 15% universal tariff — positive signal
EU Services PMI Final 51.9 (in-line), EU Unemployment 6.1% (beat)
ECB Lagarde speaks today — energy shock policy guidance key

GBP.summary: GBP/USD traded 1.3303-1.3403, briefly rising above its 100 DMA (1.3398) before fading. Gilts bounced +80 ticks from Tuesday's collapse, but remain well below the week's 93.55 open. Goldman Sachs pushed next BoE cut to April (from March), projecting July and November thereafter as energy shock fades — this is a modest hawkish repricing for GBP. Trump criticised UK PM Starmer for declining to join offensive strikes. UK has no domestic data today — entirely driven by geopolitics and Iran war narrative. GBP remains structurally bearish: energy importer, weak growth (OBR 1.1%), BoE on cutting path.
drivers: GBP/USD 1.3303-1.3403 — briefly above 100 DMA (1.3398), then faded
Goldman Sachs: BoE April cut (from March), then July, November
Gilts bounced +80 ticks from collapse — but still well below week open
Trump criticises Starmer for not joining Iran strikes — political pressure
No UK data today — geopolitics and Iran narrative drive price action

JPY.summary: JPY maintains its haven bid with USD/JPY choppy in a 156.85-157.86 band. BoJ sources (Bloomberg) confirm April rate hike remains on the table — little chance of March hike but April not ruled out. BoJ Governor Ueda reiterated wages expected to rise broadly in 2026 wage negotiations and annual real wages to gradually turn positive. Japan sold JPY 530bln 30yr JGBs with solid demand (b/c 3.66x). The energy inflation dilemma persists — higher oil = inflationary support for hikes, but Hormuz closure = growth headwind. Net: JPY benefits from haven demand + BoJ hike optionality.
drivers: BoJ sources (Bloomberg): April hike not ruled out, March unlikely
BoJ Ueda: wages rising broadly in 2026, real wages to turn positive
USD/JPY 156.85-157.86 — haven bid intact, approaching intervention zone
Japan 30yr JGB auction solid (b/c 3.66x) — demand for JGBs healthy
Energy inflation dilemma: oil spike supports hike case, growth risk delays

AUD.summary: AUD staged a recovery as China's NPC set 2026 GDP target at 4.5%-5.0% with CNY 800bln new policy tools — bullish for AUD via China demand channel. PBoC USD/CNY fix at strongest in ~3 years is a direct positive for AUD. ASX 200 led higher by tech strength, but commodity sectors capped gains. Australian Trade Balance Jan came in at AUD 2.6bln (vs 3.9bln exp) — exports contracted -0.9%, a mild negative. However, the China stimulus narrative dominates. Antipodeans faded some gains into the European session. Key risk: Iran war escalation could reverse China-driven gains rapidly.
drivers: China NPC: GDP 4.5%-5.0% target, CNY 800bln new policy tools — AUD positive
PBoC USD/CNY fix at strongest in ~3 years — direct AUD support
ASX 200 higher — tech led, commodity sectors capped gains
AU Trade Balance Jan AUD 2.6bln (vs 3.9bln exp, miss) — exports -0.9%
Iran war escalation risk: key tail risk for AUD recovery

CAD.summary: CAD's energy windfall narrative is being tested as crude prices fell on the Iran-CIA peace rumour (WTI range $73.28-77.23, Brent $80.30-84.48). BoC Governor Macklem spoke Wednesday (16:30 Frankfurt) — no major new guidance; focus on energy shock impact on Canadian economy. Canadian PM Carney met with business leaders in Sydney to forge new energy/AI partnerships. Labour Productivity Q4 -0.1% (in-line). CAD remains caught between energy windfall (positive) and weak domestic growth (Q4 GDP -0.6% annualized). Weekly Bias is Neutral — no High Conviction alignment possible.
drivers: Crude fell on Iran-CIA peace rumour (later denied) — WTI $73-77 range
BoC Macklem spoke Wed — no major new guidance on rate path
Canadian PM Carney: new energy/AI partnerships in Sydney
Labour Productivity Q4 -0.1% (in-line) — weak domestic backdrop
Weekly Bias Neutral — no High Conviction Setup alignment

CHF.summary: CHF received a dual boost: Swiss CPI Feb +0.6% MoM (vs +0.5% exp, hot) and +0.1% YoY (vs -0.1% exp, above zero) — this reduces SNB easing pressure and supports CHF. SNB Vice Chair reiterated readiness to intervene in FX markets, but EUR/CHF only saw modest upside on the comment. USD/CHF rebounded from 0.7790 lows after the hot CPI print. SNB March 19 meeting is now a key event — hot CPI reduces probability of a cut. CHF remains the premier haven in G10 with Switzerland's energy independence and low inflation profile intact.
drivers: Swiss CPI Feb +0.6% MoM (vs +0.5% exp, hot) — reduces SNB cut probability
Swiss CPI YoY +0.1% (vs -0.1% exp) — above zero, SNB meeting March 19 key
SNB Vice Chair: ready to intervene in FX markets
USD/CHF rebounded from 0.7790 lows after hot CPI
Premier haven: energy independence + low inflation = cleanest G10 safe haven

NZD.summary: NZD was steadier and set to end the European session firmer alongside AUD, lifted by China's NPC GDP target (4.5%-5.0%) and CNY 800bln policy tools. China is NZD's largest trading partner — any China stimulus signal is directly positive for NZD. However, the recovery is fragile: RBNZ dovish pivot remains intact, and NZD is the first currency to be cut in risk-off episodes. The Iran war remains the dominant tail risk. NZD has no domestic data today — entirely driven by China headlines and global risk sentiment.
drivers: China NPC: GDP 4.5%-5.0%, CNY 800bln policy tools — NZD positive via trade
NZD steadier — set to end European session firmer alongside AUD
RBNZ dovish pivot intact — OCR to remain at current levels near term
No domestic data today — entirely China and risk sentiment driven
Iran war escalation: key tail risk for NZD recovery

bonds.name: US 10Y Treasury
bonds.summary: 10yr UST futures remained subdued after retreating Wednesday amid higher yields and better-than-expected ISM Services (56.1) and ADP (63K). Iran-CIA peace rumour (later denied) provided brief support, but the inflationary angle of the war dominates. Fed Miran: 100bps of easing appropriate in 2026, March cut appropriate. Fed Hammack: hold rates for 'quite some time'. Today's Unemployment Claims are the key NFP preview catalyst.
drivers: USTs subdued — ISM Services 56.1 and ADP 63K beat pushed yields higher
Iran-CIA peace rumour (denied) provided brief support — quickly faded
Fed Miran: 100bps easing in 2026, March cut appropriate
Fed Hammack: hold rates steady for 'quite some time'
Unemployment Claims today (14:30) — NFP preview, key yield catalyst

bonds.name: German 10Y Bund
bonds.summary: Bunds held in the upper half of 128.80-129.32 range, managing to stay in the green despite higher energy prices stoking inflationary concerns. Iran-CIA peace rumour (later denied) briefly supported Bunds via lower nat gas (Dutch TTF -10% front-month). Morgan Stanley now expects ECB to hold all 2026 — stagflationary signal. ECB Lagarde speaks today — key for Bund direction.
drivers: Bunds in 128.80-129.32 range — managed to stay green
Dutch TTF -10% front-month on Iran peace rumour (later denied)
Morgan Stanley: ECB holds all 2026 — stagflation risk
ECB Lagarde speaks today — key for Bund direction
Germany sold EUR 0.96bln 2033 Green Bund (b/c 1.76x, weak demand)

bonds.name: Japan 10Y JGB
bonds.summary: 10yr JGB futures tracked the downside in global peers amid rising yield environment. Japan sold JPY 530bln 30yr JGBs with solid demand (b/c 3.66x, prev. 3.64x), average yield 3.398% (prev. 3.615%) — lower yield accepted, positive for JGBs. BoJ Ueda: wages rising broadly, real wages to turn positive. April hike not ruled out per Bloomberg sources.
drivers: JGB futures tracked global peers lower — rising yield environment
Japan 30yr JGB auction solid: b/c 3.66x, yield 3.398% (lower than prev.)
BoJ Ueda: wages rising broadly in 2026, real wages to turn positive
BoJ sources: April hike not ruled out, March unlikely
USD/JPY 156.85-157.86 — approaching intervention zone

bonds.name: UK 10Y Gilt
bonds.summary: Gilts bounced +80 ticks from Tuesday's collapse, gapping higher by 37 ticks at the open and climbing to 92.34 peak. However, the recovery left Gilts shy of Tuesday's 92.47 opening and well below the week's 93.55 open. Goldman Sachs pushed next BoE cut to April (from March). UK has no domestic data today — Gilt direction driven by global risk sentiment and Iran war narrative.
drivers: Gilts bounced +80 ticks from collapse — gapped higher 37 ticks at open
Recovery to 92.34 peak — shy of Tue 92.47 open, well below 93.55 week open
Goldman Sachs: BoE April cut (from March), then July, November
No UK data today — Gilt direction driven by geopolitics
UK 2yr yield remains elevated after biggest daily rise since May 2025
